title: dactylorhiza romana subsp. guimaraesii
intro: Dactylorhiza romana subsp. guimaraesii is a rare subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula, specifically in the northern part of Portugal. The medicinal properties of this plant involve its use as a potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, particularly in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza romana subsp. guimaraesii include its ability to aid in the healing process, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create topical creams, salves, and infusions that are applied directly to the affected area.
